status: build-phase demonstrator, internal, not for publication
prepared_for: Office of Bevan Eatts MLA (Nationals WA, Member for Warren-Blackwood)
one_line: One public, permanent, machine-readable register of what government was told, what it promised, and what it did.

discipline.direction: Radical transparency pointed upward at power, not downward at the public. Holds recommendations, not people.
discipline.robodebt_guardrail: No personal data, no algorithm over any citizen, no automated decision about anyone. The opposite of Robodebt, on purpose.
discipline.enforcement: Check the ledger before you write the cheque: any business case to commission external research/consultancy must first cite the ledger and show the question is genuinely unanswered, or state on the record why it departs from what was already recommended.
discipline.honesty_rule: Every figure carries a source and a confidence flag. Figures are never summed, each has its own source. Entries with status UNVERIFIED or confidence LOW are withheld from public use (public_use=false) and flagged HOLD.

A.name: Open Recommendations
A.blurb: An inquiry or audit answer government was given, often paid for, and whether it was acted on.

B.name: They Were Warned
B.blurb: A documented warning, harm that followed, and the costly patch. Some entries are live and flagged unverified.

C.name: Consultation Integrity
C.blurb: A “we consulted” claim turned into a checkable record. Most WA cases here are not yet verified, shown honestly.

D.name: The Receipts
D.blurb: A specific consultant or IT-spend case, with the dollar figure and the outcome.

status_values.PARTIAL: Partly acted on; material commitments still open.
status_values.IGNORED: Answer/warning on the record; not acted on.
status_values.RECURRING: The same failure or finding recurs year after year.
status_values.UNVERIFIED: Not yet confirmed to a primary source, or a live matter not yet decided.

confidence_values.HIGH: Confirmed to a primary source; safe to use.
confidence_values.MEDIUM: Sourced but with a caveat noted in `notes`; confirm at point of use.
confidence_values.LOW: Weak or single-thread sourcing; must not be used publicly until strengthened.

self_commitments.text: Every figure in this project carries a source and a confidence flag.
self_commitments.detail: Self-imposed, in force across all working docs

self_commitments.text: Publish the pilot's baseline before it starts, no baseline, no claim.
Publish a 12-month outcomes evaluation, the thing Target 120 never got.
Lead with the hardest numbers; concede the soft ones in the open.

entries.title: Robodebt Royal Commission, 57 recs, years to act
entries.what: 57 recommendations on lawful, human-supervised automated decision-making; government accepted or accepted-in-principle 56.
entries.outcome: As of late 2024 only ~28 reported fully implemented; the rest a multi-year grind that only moves while someone keeps score in public.
entries.source: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme, Final Report (Holmes AC SC), 7 Jul 2023

entries.title: Aboriginal Deaths in Custody, 339 recs, still open
entries.what: The 1991 Royal Commission made 339 recommendations; taxpayers paid for the answers decades ago.
entries.outcome: Decades on a great many remain unimplemented, with live disputes that official reviews overstated how much had been done.
entries.source: Royal Commission into Aboriginal Deaths in Custody (1991); ANU CIPR

entries.title: WA Auditor General: “same weaknesses, year after year”
entries.what: The annual Information Systems Audit keeps reissuing the same control-weakness recommendations to agencies.
entries.outcome: AG: agencies “still not taking action… not taking the risks seriously”, the same warning recurs 2014 to 2017 to 2024 to 2025.
entries.source: WA Auditor General, Information Systems Audit Report 2017 (Caroline Spencer)

entries.title: WA AG 2025: 65% of IT findings carried over, again
entries.what: The 2025 IS audit raised 359 control findings across 53 entities; the AG notes many “do not require large technology investments to fix.”
entries.outcome: 65% were unaddressed from prior years and 57% of the most serious remained unresolved, a failure of will, not budget.
entries.source: WA Auditor General, State Government 2025 Information Systems Audit Results

entries.title: WA AG told to track blowouts; govt called it “duplication”
entries.what: The AG recommended transparent tracking of major-project cost and time; in 2022 Finance and Treasury called more reporting “unnecessary duplication.”
entries.outcome: The AG tracked it anyway, and that same work then found $1.6bn blown out and information “patchy, inconsistent and not transparent.”
entries.source: WA Auditor General, Transparency Report - Major Projects (2022)

entries.title: Cowaramup service station, warned live, decision pending
entries.what: A 12-bay service station is before an unelected Development Assessment Panel; 100+ residents protested on the safety grounds the town's own Precinct Plan already flags.
entries.outcome: Live, decision NOT yet made. In 2017 a servo drew 297 objections and was rejected, yet a truckstop operates there now. Not an “override” yet.
entries.source: DAP application DAP/26/03096 (Apr 2026); resident protest May 2026
entries.hold_reason: HOLD: not for public use (status UNVERIFIED). Live matter, decision not yet made. Do not present as an override until the DAP determines it.
entries.notes: Live matter, decision not yet made. Do not present as an override until the DAP determines it.

entries.title: Container deposit scheme (wine), warned, launched anyway
entries.what: Friends of WA Wine put it in writing: ~$1.15 to 1.25 real cost per bottle vs a 10c refund, ~$11m/yr for under 1% litter gain, no WA glass reprocessor, WA hit a year early. They asked for a pause.
entries.outcome: Government launched on schedule; the cost lands on regional wine country. The strongest of the four, fully documented, Warren-Blackwood's own back yard.
entries.source: Friends of WA Wine submission & Minister meeting (Mar 2026)

entries.title: West Coast demersal fishing, warned 15 years, brutal patch
entries.what: The fishery ran on an “interim” plan since 2007; overfishing was on the record ~15 years while fixes stayed too small.
entries.outcome: Dec 2025: rec fishing shut 21 months, commercial demersal permanently closed, $29.2m package. Frame only as “managed too slowly”, the science was right.
entries.source: WA West Coast Demersal Scalefish management; Dec 2025 closure

entries.title: Sandalwood (FPC to DBCA), warned since 1921
entries.what: WA Forests Dept reports warned in 1921 and 1958 that harvest rates were driving sandalwood to extinction; Red-listed vulnerable in 2021, wild populations down ~90%.
entries.outcome: Control moves to DBCA from 2027 with quota quietly cut ~20%; the FPC blames bettongs and rainfall, never a century of harvest. Confirm handover scope.
entries.source: WA Forests Dept reports 1921 & 1958; IUCN Red List 2021
entries.notes: Confirm the FPC to DBCA handover scope before public use.

entries.title: CDS regional refund-point access, warned 2018
entries.what: A 2018 warning held that regional and remote communities would struggle to access container refund points.
entries.outcome: The warning is real and documented, but we could not confirm it materialised into closures or harm. Held as “worth checking,” not proven.
entries.source: 2018 regional/remote refund-point access warning
entries.hold_reason: HOLD: not for public use (status UNVERIFIED, confidence LOW). Warning documented but not confirmed to have materialised into harm. Worth checking, not proven.
entries.notes: Warning documented but not confirmed to have materialised into harm. Worth checking, not proven.

entries.title: Aboriginal Cultural Heritage Act 2021, rushed, repealed in 5 weeks
entries.what: The Act passed both houses in ~5 weeks and came into force 1 July 2023; a ~29,000-signature petition sought delay.
entries.outcome: Repeal announced five weeks after commencement. A rushed process that failed landholders and Aboriginal people alike. A process failure, told evenhandedly.
entries.source: Aboriginal Cultural Heritage Act 2021 (WA); repeal 8 Aug 2023

entries.title: Native-forest logging ban, industry “blindsided”
entries.what: Government claimed consultation on ending native-forest logging.
entries.outcome: Timber industry and forest towns said they were “blindsided,” learning of a livelihood-reshaping decision through the announcement.
entries.source: 6PR, industry “blindsided” by native forestry ban

entries.title: WA planning reform (2023), claim not yet tested
entries.what: A large reform with extensive official consultation pages; the government presents a thorough process.
entries.outcome: Searching did not surface evidence the windows were tokenistic. Whether lived experience matched needs the primary-source window dates and submission mix. Unverified.
entries.source: Planning and Development Amendment Act 2023 (WA)
entries.hold_reason: HOLD: not for public use (status UNVERIFIED, confidence LOW). Need primary-source consultation window dates and submission mix before any use.
entries.notes: Need primary-source consultation window dates and submission mix before any use.

entries.title: WA local-government amalgamations, process unverified
entries.what: WA has real history here (the metropolitan program that collapsed after ratepayer rejection is the obvious candidate).
entries.outcome: Specific consultation-process facts could not be verified in this pass. Needs dedicated primary-source work before any public use.
entries.source: WA local-government reform / amalgamation program
entries.hold_reason: HOLD: not for public use (status UNVERIFIED, confidence LOW). Needs dedicated primary-source work before any public use.
entries.notes: Needs dedicated primary-source work before any public use.

entries.title: Environment Online / COVID-era changes, unverified
entries.what: Plausible candidates for box-ticking consultation on environmental-approval reforms and COVID-era regulatory changes.
entries.outcome: Each needs its real window, announcement and stakeholder mix checked, not assumed. Plausible but unverified.
entries.source: WA Environment Online / environmental-approval & COVID-era reforms
entries.hold_reason: HOLD: not for public use (status UNVERIFIED, confidence LOW). Placeholder candidates. Each needs its real window, announcement and stakeholder mix checked.
entries.notes: Placeholder candidates. Each needs its real window, announcement and stakeholder mix checked.

entries.title: Deloitte $440k AI report with fabricated citations
entries.what: DEWR paid Deloitte $440,000 to review the automated welfare-penalty system.
entries.outcome: The report carried a dozen-plus fabricated citations and a made-up Federal Court quote, produced with GPT-4o; caught by an academic. Deloitte repaid just $97,587, under a quarter of the fee.
entries.source: DEWR Assurance Review; AusTender CN4118426; Sen. Pocock (Oct 2025)

entries.title: PwC tax leaks, partner banned, 12 Senate recs
entries.what: A PwC partner used confidential Treasury information, received while advising government on an incoming anti-avoidance law, to help clients sidestep it.
entries.outcome: The Tax Practitioners Board terminated his registration; three federal inquiries followed; the Senate committee made 12 recommendations on consulting integrity.
entries.source: Tax Practitioners Board decision; Senate Finance & Public Administration Cttee

entries.title: ATO IT contracts blew out $19.06m to $88.11m
entries.what: Six ATO advisor contracts for IT managed services rose from an approved $19.06m to $88.11m; contractors supply roughly half the ATO's annual IT spend.
entries.outcome: The ANAO found the value-for-money demonstration “deficient”; eight managed-services contracts worth ~$2.5bn over ten years underpin the dependency.
entries.source: ANAO, ATO Procurement of IT Managed Services (performance audit)

entries.title: WA's $1.6bn IT blowout, 10 major projects
entries.what: The AG examined 10 major State IT projects estimated at $2.6bn.
entries.outcome: They rose to at least $4.2bn, a $1.6bn / 62% overrun; half at least doubled, 8 of 10 late by 4 to 78 months. $4.2bn is a floor.
entries.source: WA Auditor General, 2025 Transparency Report - Major IT Projects
entries.notes: $4.2bn is a floor, not a total. Never sum ledger figures.

entries.title: Queensland Health / IBM payroll, $98m to ~$1.2bn
entries.what: A payroll system project contracted around $98m.
entries.outcome: Blew out to ~$1.2bn over eight years; the Commission of Inquiry called it in the “front rank of failures… may be the worst” and found a “distinct partiality for IBM.”
entries.source: Queensland Health Payroll Commission of Inquiry (Chesterman), 2013

entries.title: WA HRMIS health payroll (Deloitte/SAP), $220m and rising
entries.what: In 2022 Deloitte/SAP won a ~$220m contract to build WA Health's HR/payroll system.
entries.outcome: Now $274.8m, up $39.2m in a single budget, ~90% spent and still drawing funds.
entries.source: WA Health/DoF HRMIS contract disclosures; iTnews (2022)
entries.notes: $274.8m is a moving figure; re-confirm at point of use.

entries.title: GovNext-ICT, the $65m savings that never came
entries.what: The GovNext-ICT program was sold on projected savings of $65m.
entries.outcome: The Department of Premier and Cabinet accepted the AG's finding: “little doubt that the projected $65 million savings… will not be achieved.”
entries.source: WA Auditor General, Implementation of the GovNext-ICT Program

entries.title: Office of Shared Services, ~$370m to unwind
entries.what: WA's Office of Shared Services was sold as a savings program.
entries.outcome: Scrapped as a costly write-off: ~$370m to unwind after ~$258m had already been spent. Claimed savings became a loss.
entries.source: iTnews (2011), “WA to pay $370m to scrap shared services”

entries.title: WA's consultant shadow, $7.3m reported vs $154.7m real
entries.what: WA disclosed just $7.3m of whole-of-government “consultants” (six months to June 2023).
entries.outcome: One agency, Health Support Services, spent $154.7m on “computer services” in a single year. The real spend hides under labels like “computer” and “professional” services.
entries.source: Report on Consultants (tabled paper 2905); 2024-25 agency annual reports
entries.notes: The $7.3m-vs-$154.7m contrast is a moving figure; re-confirm at point of use.

headline.note: Counts describe the register; dollar figures are never summed.
